I'm not very familiar with this code, but I guess it is important to understand what is the actual problem being fixed here?

If the client does not have enough "dirty_max_pages" to complete the write, doesn't it make sense to *throttle* the write on the client instead of continuing to send more and more RPCs to the server?

If the writes are hitting the dirty limit, it makes sense to block the writer and flush out the older pages, rather than sending many more sync write RPCs that are poorly formed and may further slow down the server performance...

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2:
> > 
> > Are you sure this is the cause of LU-17272?  It's not obvious to me that's the case, and I'm not sure we should restrict DIO with max dirty pages...
> 
> It could be caused by it, and the write will be converted to normal DIO instead of
> parallel DIO if the grant is not enough, it will make individual RPCs synchronously,
> IMHO, the dirty_max limit is something like the grant and should be check at the same time?

Ah, OK - yeah, I see what you mean.  Looking at the code, we increment max dirty pages for DIO - we do it when getting grant - but don't limit by it.  Yes, that's no good and would lead to that warning message.

All right, this looks good to me.  I have some thoughts about how we manage dirty data in general - the 2 GiB per OSC limit is probably too small - but that shouldn't stop us from fixing this bug.

commit.subject: LU-17272 osc: check cl_dirty_max_pages
commit.message:
LU-17272 osc: check cl_dirty_max_pages

In osc_queue_sync_pages, the client_obd->cl_dirty_max_pages
should also be checked along with the grant check.
